title: Call: A call option gives the holder the obligation to purchase...
summary: Distinguish the right granted by a call option from the obligation in futures contracts.
prompt: A call option gives the holder the obligation to purchase the underlying asset at a specified price.
solution: False; a call option grants the holder the right, but not the obligation, to purchase an asset at a specified strike price before or at expiration, unlike futures contracts where there is an obligation to transact.
